epic_sepsis_model.description: ML model that predicts sepsis risk from EHR data and alerts clinical staff. Shown to produce high false-positive rates in some populations.
known_issues: Published studies show higher false positive rates in Black patients
Retrospective validation may not reflect real-world performance
Alert fatigue documented at multiple health systems
governance_requirements: Pre-deployment validation on local population required
Human clinician must review all alerts
Bias monitoring required quarterly
Patient disclosure required in TX, CA, CO

nuance_dax.description: Ambient AI that listens to patient-physician conversations and generates clinical notes automatically.
known_issues: Audio recording of patient conversations raises consent questions
AI-generated notes can contain hallucinations if audio is unclear
Some patients unaware they are being recorded
governance_requirements: Explicit patient consent for ambient recording required
Physician must review and attest all AI-generated notes
BAA with Microsoft/Nuance must be current
Audio data retention policy required

google_deepmind_streams.description: AI system for detecting acute kidney injury from blood test results. Generates alerts for clinical teams.
known_issues: DeepMind UK NHS data sharing raised major privacy concerns
Algorithm performance varies significantly by patient demographics
Google Cloud data processing agreements must be carefully reviewed
governance_requirements: Google BAA and Cloud DPA must both be current
Clinical validation on local population required
Data minimization principles must be documented
Impact assessment required in CO

optum_claims_ai.display_name: Optum Claims and Prior Authorization AI
optum_claims_ai.description: AI system used in prior authorization and claims processing. Subject to Congressional and DOJ scrutiny for high denial rates.
known_issues: Class action lawsuit filed 2024 alleging algorithm denies medically necessary care
High denial rates for elderly patients documented
DOJ antitrust investigation ongoing as of 2025
nH Predict algorithm specifically criticized for post-acute care denials
governance_requirements: Explainability requirement for all denials
Human review required for all denied claims
CMS reporting required for Medicare/Medicaid denials
Patient appeal pathway must be clearly documented

viz_ai.description: FDA-cleared AI that analyzes CT scans to detect stroke and cardiovascular conditions, alerting specialists via mobile.
known_issues: False negative rate in certain anatomical variants
Time-critical alerts can create clinical workflow pressure
governance_requirements: FDA 510(k) clearance documentation must be on file
Radiologist or neurologist must confirm all alerts
Local validation study recommended
Incident reporting to FDA and HHS required for adverse events

chatgpt_clinical.description: Consumer ChatGPT used informally by clinical staff for documentation, differential diagnosis, or patient communication. NOT covered by BAA. Major HIPAA risk if PHI entered.
known_issues: No BAA available for consumer tier — entering PHI is a HIPAA violation
Hallucination rate in clinical contexts is undocumented
No audit trail for clinical decisions
No institutional governance
OpenAI may use inputs for training
governance_requirements: IMMEDIATE: Policy required prohibiting entry of PHI into consumer LLMs
Staff training required
If enterprise tier with BAA — additional governance still required
Audit of current usage recommended

azure_openai_clinical.description: Enterprise Azure OpenAI deployment for clinical applications. BAA-eligible. More governable than consumer ChatGPT.
known_issues: Still requires custom governance layer
Model updates can change behavior unexpectedly
Prompt injection risks in clinical workflows
governance_requirements: Azure BAA must be current
Data residency must be configured to US regions
Prompt logging and audit trail required
Clinical validation required before deployment

ambient_clinical_intelligence.description: Voice-based ambient AI tools for clinical note generation. Fast-growing category with variable governance maturity.
known_issues: Patient consent for audio capture varies by vendor implementation
AI note accuracy varies significantly by clinical specialty
Billing code suggestions from AI notes raise compliance questions
governance_requirements: Patient notice required before ambient recording
Physician attestation workflow required
BAA with specific vendor required
Note review policy required before billing submission

ehr_predictive_analytics.display_name: EHR Native Predictive Analytics (Epic, Cerner, etc.)
ehr_predictive_analytics.description: AI models embedded within EHR platforms (readmission risk, deterioration alerts, etc.). Often deployed without separate governance review.
known_issues: Hospitals often activate without realizing AI-specific governance applies
Models trained on majority-population data may underperform for minorities
Alert fatigue from multiple simultaneous predictive models
governance_requirements: Inventory all activated AI features in EHR contracts
Ensure original EHR BAA covers AI feature data usage
Clinical validation recommended before activation
Bias monitoring program required in CO, recommended everywhere

radiology_ai_cad.description: AI systems that analyze radiological images to flag abnormalities. FDA-cleared tools are available but governance requirements remain.
known_issues: FDA clearance does not eliminate need for local governance
Performance varies by imaging equipment and patient population
Radiologist over-reliance on AI flags documented
governance_requirements: FDA clearance documentation required on file
Local clinical validation study recommended
Radiologist must maintain independent judgment
Adverse event reporting to FDA + HHS required

billing_coding_ai.description: AI tools that suggest or automate ICD/CPT billing codes from clinical documentation. High risk for fraudulent billing if not governed.
known_issues: AI upcoding risk — model may suggest higher-severity codes
CMS actively auditing AI-assisted billing for Medicare fraud
HHS OIG has flagged AI billing tools in fraud risk bulletins
governance_requirements: Human coder review required before claim submission
Audit trail of AI suggestions vs final codes required
Regular compliance review of AI code suggestion accuracy
OIG compliance program should specifically address AI billing tools

critical.description: Immediate patient safety or major legal risk. Requires immediate action.

high.description: Significant compliance gap with potential for penalties or patient harm.

medium.description: Compliance gap that should be addressed. Limited immediate risk.

low.description: Best practice gap. No immediate legal or safety risk.

high_risk_indicators: Staff reporting use of consumer AI chatbots for clinical tasks
Unvetted AI browser extensions on clinical workstations
Undeclared AI features in existing vendor contracts
Consumer-grade AI used for discharge summaries or care plans
Personal devices with AI apps accessing clinical systems
common_shadow_ai_tools: Consumer voice assistants in clinical areas
Undeclared EHR AI modules activated by vendor
